# naming-vs-substance

When mechanisms are named for what they claim to measure (e.g., "AdaptabilityGate") but actually test something else (e.g., coherence), diagnostic errors cascade—optimization efforts target the wrong dimension, leaving the actual goal untouched. The pattern appears across gates, tests, and APIs: 命名跟實質落差 causes measurement failures, where metrics move in adjacent dimensions rather than the intended one. The antidote is pre-implementation validation: clarify what failure means, predict which dimension moves, and verify the mechanism truly addresses the real X before naming it.
